compiled_state.title: As a downstream user, I see updated documentation that describes the parity-uplifted CI architecture and the new ci.dso_plugin_version config key
compiled_state.author: Test
compiled_state.description:
## Description

**What**: Update plugin documentation to remove all DSO_LLM_TIER references, document the new classifier-driven tier selection, document the new ci.dso_plugin_version config key (Tier 2 of the resolution chain), and document the auto-detect runner mode.

**Why**: SC7 of Epic C — without these doc updates, onboarding and CONFIGURATION-REFERENCE.md describe a CI architecture that no longer exists. Downstream developers reading the docs would set DSO_LLM_TIER (which has no effect) and miss the new ci.dso_plugin_version key.

**Scope**:
- IN: edit plugins/dso/docs/CONFIGURATION-REFERENCE.md and plugins/dso/skills/onboarding/SKILL.md; remove all DSO_LLM_TIER references; add documentation for classifier-driven tier selection, the new ci.dso_plugin_version config key, and the auto-detect runner mode (local-checkout vs fetched-assets); if any CI job is renamed during the port, also update .github/required-checks.txt and verify validate-required-checks.sh passes.
- OUT: CLAUDE.md architecture section update (covered by Story 9); edits to other docs that don't reference DSO_LLM_TIER or the new keys.

## Done Definitions

- When this story is complete, plugins/dso/docs/CONFIGURATION-REFERENCE.md and plugins/dso/skills/onboarding/SKILL.md no longer contain any references to DSO_LLM_TIER (verified via grep returning zero matches).
  ← Satisfies: "remove all DSO_LLM_TIER references"
- When this story is complete, both files document the new classifier-driven tier selection (per-PR, automatic) and the new ci.dso_plugin_version config key (Tier 2 of the 3-tier resolution chain).
  ← Satisfies: "replace with documentation of the new classifier-driven tier selection plus the new ci.dso_plugin_version config key"
- When this story is complete, CONFIGURATION-REFERENCE.md documents the auto-detect runner mode behavior (local-checkout vs fetched-assets, the marker file plugins/dso/.dso-source-of-truth, the $DSO_ASSETS_DIR env var contract).
  ← Satisfies: documentation must cover Story 5's auto-detect contract
- When this story is complete, if any CI job was renamed during the Epic C port, .github/required-checks.txt has been updated and validate-required-checks.sh passes — verified in-session.
  ← Satisfies: "if any CI job is renamed during the port, .github/required-checks.txt is updated and validate-required-checks.sh passes — verified in-session"
- unit tests written and passing for all new or modified logic (e.g., a structural test that DSO_LLM_TIER no longer appears in the documented files)

## Considerations

- [Maintainability] Documentation for the auto-detect mode (Story 5) requires Story 5 to be implemented first — otherwise this story would document an unimplemented behavior. The dependency on Story 5 enforces correct ordering.

## Escalation Policy

**Escalation policy**: Proceed unless a significant assumption is required to continue — one that could send the implementation in the wrong direction. Escalate only when genuinely blocked without a reasonable inference. Document all assumptions made without escalating.
